chat.prompt: What are Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 emissions?

chat.description: Must describe LCA in sustainability context

chat.prompt: How do companies report carbon footprint?
Our product inventory lists components with carbon values in kg CO2e. How should I interpret those LCA numbers when prioritizing reductions?
chat.description: Must tie LCA quantities (e.g. kg CO2e) to interpretation and action

chat.prompt: What is a functional unit in life cycle assessment, and why does it matter when comparing two products' carbon footprints?
chat.description: Must explain functional unit and comparability of LCA results

chat.prompt: We ship finished goods to retailers by road and sometimes air. How should transport and logistics emissions appear in Scope 3 reporting?
chat.description: Must connect freight/logistics to Scope 3 categories or reporting

chat.prompt: For supply chain reporting, how do typical carbon intensities of road, rail, sea, and air freight compare at a high level?
chat.description: Must mention multiple transport modes and carbon context

report.prompt: Generate a sustainability report for a fictional F&B company called GreenBite for FY2025.
report.description: Must return valid JSON with all required keys and substantive content

report.prompt: Write a carbon report for a retail company focusing on Scope 3.
report.description: Must return valid JSON with reportTitle, boardStatement, pillars, futureTargets

report.prompt: Generate a sustainability report for a fictional consumer goods distributor called NorthRelay for FY2025. Emphasize Scope 3 transport: inbound freight from suppliers, warehousing energy at a low level, and outbound distribution to customers by road and intermodal rail. Include targets to shift tonne-kilometres from road to rail where feasible.
report.description: Must return valid JSON; content should reflect transport, logistics, and distribution themes

report.prompt: Write a sustainability and carbon report for a fictional office-furniture maker called DeskForm for FY2025. Ground the narrative in product-level thinking: material sourcing (steel, wood, textiles), manufacturing energy, packaging, and transport to the end customer. Mention LCA or product carbon footprint concepts in the environmental analysis sections.
report.description: Must return valid JSON; environmental pillars should engage LCA / PCF-style themes
